info.title: Global News Aggregation API
info.description: A comprehensive news aggregation platform providing diverse global perspectives

get.description:
Return configured source categories.

The frontend calls this as a top-level endpoint.

Paginated article endpoint with cursor-based navigation.

Cursor pagination is more efficient than offset for large datasets:
- Consistent performance regardless of page number
- No "skipping" issues when new data is inserted
- Better index utilization

Supports filtering by:
- category: Single category filter
- source: Single source filter (legacy)
- sources: Comma-separated list for multi-source filtering
- search: Text search in title and summary

Returns:
    PaginatedResponse with articles, cursors, and metadata

Paginated endpoint using in-memory cache (faster for frequently accessed data).
Uses offset pagination since cache is in-memory array.

Best for:
- Initial page loads
- Common category filters
- Real-time updates

Lightweight recent articles endpoint for historical paging.

Uses keyset pagination and avoids total counts for faster queries.

get.description:
Local Lens feature: Get news for a specific country.

Args:
    code: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., "US", "GB", "CN")
    view:
        - "internal": Articles FROM this country (source_country = code)
        - "external": Articles ABOUT this country (country mentioned in content)
                      Currently uses source country != code as approximation
    limit: Maximum number of articles to return
    offset: Pagination offset

Returns:
    Paginated list of articles matching the criteria

schema.title: Response Get News For Country News Country  Code  Get

get.description:
Get comprehensive debug report for agentic debugging tools.

This endpoint returns everything needed to diagnose issues:
- Performance summary with component stats
- Active streams and their state
- Recent events (last 50)
- Slow operations detected
- Hang suspects
- AI-generated recommendations

Use this as the primary entry point for debugging sessions.

schema.title: Response Get Debug Report Debug Logs Report Get

get.description:
Get recent debug events.

Event types include:
- request_start, request_end, request_error
- stream_start, stream_event, stream_end, stream_error
- db_query_start, db_query_end, db_query_error
- cache_hit, cache_miss, cache_update
- rss_fetch_start, rss_fetch_end, rss_fetch_error
- performance_warning, bottleneck_detected, hang_suspected

schema.title: Response Get Debug Events Debug Logs Events Get

get.description:
Get detailed info about active streams being traced.

post.description:
Return a short one-paragraph definition for a highlighted term.

Expects JSON: {"term": "Janet Yellen", "context": "US economics"}

Start a background refresh job.

Returns a job ID that can be used to stream progress via GET /jobs/{job_id}/stream.
This separates job initiation from progress streaming for better SSE handling.

get.description:
Stream progress events for a specific job via SSE.

Events follow SSE spec with proper id, retry, and data fields.
Client can resume with Last-Event-ID header.
Get current status of a job without streaming.

SSE stream for lightweight cache invalidation events.

Events:
- invalidate: New content available, client should refetch
- refresh_started: A refresh job has started
- refresh_complete: A refresh job has completed

This does NOT stream article data. Use /api/news/page for actual content.
Get current updates stream status.

schema.title: Response Get Updates Status Updates Status Get

get.description:
Proxy external images to avoid mixed content blocking.

Features:
- Fetches HTTP images and serves over current protocol
- Validates content-type is image/*
- Caches to disk with hash-based filenames
- Sets proper caching headers (24h cache, 1h stale-while-revalidate)
- Returns appropriate error codes for failures

parameters.name: url

schema.description: URL of the image to proxy
schema.title: Url

parameters.description: URL of the image to proxy

get.description: Get trending topic clusters based on velocity and diversity.
Get breaking news clusters showing unusual activity spikes.

Get all active topic clusters from the latest pre-computed snapshot.

Served exclusively from the Postgres snapshot written by the background
cluster computation worker — no ChromaDB call at request time.

Returns status="initializing" with an empty cluster list when no snapshot
exists yet (e.g. shortly after first startup).
Get detailed information about a specific topic cluster.
Get overall trending system statistics for debugging and monitoring.

get.description: Check verification agent status and configuration.

schema.title: Response Get Verification Status Api Verification Status Get

post.description:
Verify claims from research output.

Request body:
- query: The original research query
- main_answer: The research agent's response text
- main_findings: Optional list of structured findings

Returns verification result with:
- overall_confidence: 0.0-1.0 score
- verified_claims: List of claims with confidence levels
- sources: Dictionary of source information
- markdown_report: Formatted report with footnotes

Stream verification progress as Server-Sent Events.

Events:
- type: "started" - Verification begun
- type: "claim" - Individual claim verified
- type: "progress" - Progress update (0.0-1.0)
- type: "complete" - Final result
- type: "error" - Error occurred

Verify claims and return structured JSON response.

Returns a summary-focused format for frontend widgets.

schema.title: Response Verify Claims Json Api Verification Verify Json Post

delete.description:
Clear expired verification cache entries.

Also cleans up stale sandbox workspaces.

post.description:
Compare two articles and return detailed analysis.

Performs:
- Entity extraction and comparison
- Keyword frequency analysis
- Text similarity calculation
- Visual diff generation

Args:
    request: ComparisonRequest with content and titles of both articles

Returns:
    ComparisonResponse with comprehensive analysis

get.description:
Get blind spots analysis for a specific source.

Identifies topics that this source is NOT covering while other sources are.

Args:
    source_name: Name of the news source
    days: Analysis period in days (1-90)

Returns:
    Blind spots analysis with coverage gaps

Get topics where major sources have blind spots.

Identifies stories that many sources are covering, but some major sources
are not reporting on.

Args:
    min_sources: Minimum number of sources covering topic to analyze

Returns:
    List of topics with blind spots and severity ratings

schema.title: Response Get Topic Blind Spots Blindspots Topics Get

get.description:
Get comprehensive coverage report for all sources.

Provides:
- Source rankings by coverage ratio
- Systemic blind spots (topics many sources miss)
- Underperforming sources (coverage ratio < 50%)

Args:
    days: Analysis period in days (7-90)

Returns:
    Comprehensive coverage report

post.description:
Trigger update of daily coverage statistics.

Updates SourceCoverageStats table with today's data.
Should be called periodically (e.g., daily) by a scheduler.

Returns:
    Update results with count of sources updated

schema.title: Response Update Coverage Stats Blindspots Update Stats Post
